title: The Incredible Freedom of Not Trying to Look Good
description: A new book explores the benefits of accepting ugliness instead of trying to change it.
author: Ilana Masad

title: The New (And Slightly Smelly) Center of the AI Boom
description: San Francisco’s brightest minds are stuffing themselves into hacker houses.
author: Matteo Wong

title: What AI Will Do to Art
description: Holly Herndon and Mat Dryhurst believe the future doesn’t have to belong to slop.
author: Spencer Kornhaber

title: A False Pretense of Judicial Modesty
description: The Supreme Court is remaking the law while claiming to preserve the status quo.
author: Duncan Hosie

title: Hegseth, Rubio, and Caine Had an Auto-Deleting Signal Chat
description: New records reveal that officials kept using the app, even after the president suggested they stop.
author: Michael Scherer,Missy Ryan,Nancy A. Youssef,andShane Harris

title: A Watergate Every Week
description: J. D. Vance contends that the scandal would be “a 12-hour news story” today. He’s probably correct, but the lesson isn’t what he claims.
author: David A. Graham

title: The White House Considers Granting 250 Pardons for the Nation’s Birthday
description: The idea has set off a frenzy of appeals for clemency.
author: Sarah FitzpatrickandMichael Scherer

title: Is Taylor Swift Making Veiled References?
description: Her fans are speculating about her wedding. It’s what she’s trained them to do.
author: Julie Beck

title: Trump Gets Roasted on Bill Maher’s Night
description: The Mark Twain Prize ceremony illustrated the perils of a president making everything about himself.
author: Janay Kingsberry

title: Photos: Rescue and Recovery in Venezuela
description: After a pair of strong earthquakes struck Venezuela last week, rescue teams from around the world headed toward affected areas, working with local teams to find survivors. Government sources say more
author: Alan Taylor

title: A Long-Standing Theory of Childbirth Is a Myth
description: Delivering a human baby is not uniquely difficult.
author: Katherine J. Wu

title: Trump’s Battle for Washington
description: The deeper problems with Trump’s D.C. remake
author: Adam Harris

title: The Man Who Saw AI Coming
description: Erik Brynjolfsson wants to talk with you about the future.
author: Annie Lowrey

title: Did Marcel Duchamp Ruin Art?
description: If you hate contemporary art, everything you dislike about it can be traced back to him.
author: Sebastian Smee

title: Dark Matter
author: Maya C. Popa

title: The Overlooked Reason Europe Doesn’t Have AC
description: Americans and Europeans tend to have very different ideas about physical suffering and sacrifice.
author: Thomas Chatterton Williams

title: The Democracies Can Still Triumph
description: Liberal societies need to show they can win—without destroying their own values from within.
author: Simon Sebag Montefiore

title: The ‘Two Ships’ Theory of American History
description: Can our unruly national story be squeezed into two vessels?
author: James Traub

title: Instagram Plus Is a New Low
description: Drive yourself crazy for the low price of $3.99 a month!
author: Annie Joy Williams

title: Long Live Mel Brooks
description: Happy 100th birthday to the funniest man who’s ever lived.
author: Adrienne LaFrance
